description: When launching a game with AI players, the server should spawn AI client subprocesses that connect back via WebSocket. CLI: specify human count and AI count (e.g. --humans 1 --ai 3, default 1 human 3 AI). Server assigns player IDs: humans first, then AIs. Server spawns one subprocess per AI player, passing the WebSocket URL and assigned player_id. AI subprocess connects, identifies itself, and plays using the standard protocol. Server waits for all human clients to connect before starting.
title: Server auto-spawns AI client subprocesses

close_reason: Converted pre-roll actions from procedural while loop to event-driven Init* pattern with TURN re-enqueue
description: Pre-roll actions (sell stock, auction, trade, buy/sell shop) are handled as a procedural while loop inside _handle_turn(). The spec shows these as events in the queue (INIT_SELL_STOCK, PROPOSED_TRADE_SHOP, TRADE_SHOP, etc.) with TURN(0) re-added after each action. This is especially important for trades, which require the other client to be notified of the trade request — impossible without events. Need to convert each pre-roll action into its own GameEvent with the TURN re-enqueue pattern.
title: Event-driven pre-roll actions (INIT_SELL_STOCK, INIT_TRADE_SHOP, etc.)

close_reason: Converted all land actions from procedural _handle_land_actions() to 10 Init* GameEvents in the pipeline. StopActionEvent handler now enqueues InitBuyShopEvent, InitBuyVacantPlotEvent, InitForcedBuyoutEvent, InitInvestEvent, InitBuyStockEvent, InitSellStockEvent, InitRenovateEvent, InitCannonEvent, VentureCardEvent, RollAgainEvent. Each has a dispatch handler in game_loop.py. All 175 tests pass.
description: Land actions (buy shop, invest, buy vacant plot, forced buyout, renovate, cannon, venture card) are handled procedurally in _handle_land_actions(). The spec shows STOP_ACTION resolving into sub-events in the queue. This needs to be evented so future complex squares can reference script events and so land actions appear in event history. Convert each land action decision point into a GameEvent.
design:
## Design: Event-Driven Land Actions

### Architecture
Each interactive land action becomes an Init* GameEvent in turn_events.py. StopActionEvent handler enqueues these instead of calling _handle_land_actions() procedurally. Each Init event has a dispatch handler in game_loop.py that does I/O via PlayerInput and enqueues the corresponding mutation event.

### New Events (all in turn_events.py, @register_event)

| Event | Fields | execute() | Handler does |
|---|---|---|---|
| InitBuyShopEvent | player_id, square_id, cost | No-op | choose_buy_shop() → BuyShopEvent |
| InitBuyVacantPlotEvent | player_id, square_id, cost, options | No-op | choose_buy_shop() + choose_vacant_plot_type() → BuyVacantPlotEvent |
| InitForcedBuyoutEvent | player_id, square_id, buyout_cost | No-op | choose_forced_buyout() → ForcedBuyoutEvent |
| InitInvestEvent | player_id, investable_shops | No-op | choose_investment() → InvestInShopEvent |
| InitBuyStockEvent | player_id | No-op | choose_stock_buy() → BuyStockEvent |
| InitSellStockEvent | player_id | No-op | choose_stock_sell() → SellStockEvent |
| InitRenovateEvent | player_id, square_id, options | No-op | choose_renovation() → RenovatePropertyEvent |
| InitCannonEvent | player_id, targets | No-op | choose_cannon_target() → WarpEvent |
| VentureCardEvent | player_id | No-op | Runs _handle_venture_card() |
| RollAgainEvent | player_id | No-op | Clears move state, removes EndTurnEvent from queue, enqueues RollEvent |

### Flow Change in _handle_stop_action()
Instead of calling _handle_land_actions(player_id, result), enqueue Init events based on result.available_actions:
- if BUY_SHOP in actions → enqueue InitBuyShopEvent(pid, sq_id, cost)
- if INVEST in actions → enqueue InitInvestEvent(pid, investable_shops)
- etc for each PlayerAction
- if venture_card → enqueue VentureCardEvent(pid)
- if roll_again → enqueue RollAgainEvent(pid)
- Victory check and liquidation stay inline (not land-action-specific, separate concern)

Queue order after StopAction: [InitBuyShop, ..., VentureCard, RollAgain, EndTurnEvent, TurnEvent(next)]

### Implementation Steps
1. Add 10 new Init events to turn_events.py (all no-op execute, registered)
2. Add _dispatch cases + handler methods in game_loop.py for each Init event
3. Move logic from _handle_land_actions() into per-event handlers
4. Replace _handle_land_actions() call in _handle_stop_action() with event enqueueing
5. Delete _handle_land_actions() method
6. Update imports
7. Run all tests

### Decision: Per-type vs generic
Per-type events (one class per action) — matches spec which shows typed events like INVEST_IN_SHOP(pid). Scripts can yield them directly.

### Note on VictoryCheck
Victory is NOT land-action-specific. It triggers on bank landing AND bankruptcy. Currently only checked on bank land — passing bank should also trigger it (separate bug fix, not this bead).
title: Event-driven land actions (buy shop, invest, venture card, etc.)

close_reason: ScriptEvent upgraded to support generator pattern. Added ScriptCommand types (Message, Decision, RollForEvent, Transaction, Warp, ExtraRoll, GrantSuit, SetFromSquare, ChooseSquare, EnqueueEvent), ScriptRunner (load_script_generator), GameLoop.run_script() + _execute_script_command(), two new PlayerInput methods (choose_script_decision, choose_any_square) implemented across all 4 input backends (Tui, Text, WebSocket, AI).
description: The current ScriptEvent uses a simple run(state, player_id) -> str function. Per the technical design doc, it must support a generator pattern where scripts can yield events (which get enqueued and executed), and then resume execution after the yielded event completes. This is critical because venture card scripts need to: trigger rolls, prompt player choices mid-script, execute multi-step effects, etc. The ScriptEvent should re-enqueue itself after each yielded event so it resumes when that event finishes. This is a prerequisite for the entire venture card system. Key yielded event types needed: ROLL_FOR_EVENT, MESSAGE, MESSAGE_WITH_DECISION, TRANSACTION, WARP, ADD_STATUS, SELL_SHOP_WITH_MOD, PLAYER_CHOOSE_SHOP, INVEST_IN_SHOP. Scripts that don't use yield (simple state mutations) should still work.
notes: Rearchitected after code review: scripts yield real GameEvents (processed via pipeline) + lightweight ScriptCommands (I/O only). No parallel type hierarchy.
title: Upgrade ScriptEvent to generator pattern

description: Stock price = value_component + fluctuation_component. Value component = 4% of total district property value (rounded nearest int). Fluctuation changes: buy/sell >= 10 shares changes by (total_price // 16 + 1). Fluctuation updates apply at end of turn. Value updates apply immediately on investment. Part of Stock Market epic.
title: Implement stock price calculation and updates

close_reason: Board renders on state_sync now, not just log messages
description: When playing 1H+3AI via play.sh, the human's TUI fails to visually update the AI's position on roughly half of the AI's individual move steps. The AI ends up in the correct final square, so the game state is correct — the issue is purely client-side rendering: intermediate steps aren't drawn. No clear pattern as to which steps are dropped. Likely a race between state_sync and log/event messages, where _refresh_board is triggered before the matching state_sync has been applied (or the refresh isn't triggered at all between syncs).
title: Human TUI skips rendering ~half of AI move steps

description: Build a converter that reads the original Fortune Street (Itadaki Street) serialized binary board files and outputs board JSON files compatible with our system. Requires reverse-engineering the binary format: square types, positions, waypoints, districts, shop values, etc.
notes: Converter working. Parses FRB binary format (I4DT/I4PL headers, 32-byte square records). Handles all known square types, bidirectional waypoints, backstreet/doorway destinations, position scaling (original 64 units = our 4 units). Tested on basic.frb and bobomb.frb. Districts counted from actual property data.
title: Binary board file converter from original game

close_reason: Removed is_start field from TurnEvent. execute() is now always idempotent. Turn header logging controlled by _turn_player_id tracker in game loop.

title: Remove TurnEvent.is_start — move bookkeeping into execute()

description:
Interactive overlay used for BUY_STOCK, SELL_STOCK, and view-only modes. Table: players as columns, districts as rows, current stock price column, cells show owned stock qty.

COVERAGE: Overlay covers ONLY the log region. Game View and player info stay visible.

INTERACTIVE: User navigates rows (districts) with arrow keys. Enter to select a district; Escape to close/cancel.

HIGHLIGHT: Currently-highlighted district row is visually highlighted. Current player's column is highlighted.

BUY/SELL PREVIEW: When overlay is in BUY or SELL mode, show a status line below the table:
  - curr price -> next price   (next price reflects pending fluctuation delta if qty >= 10)
  - curr ready cash -> next ready cash   (reflects the prospective buy/sell at chosen qty)

PRICE DISPLAY: Total price only (value+fluctuation collapsed). Pending delta is only surfaced via the curr->next preview.

INTEGRATION:
  - Plumbed into existing BUY_STOCK / SELL_STOCK InputRequest pipeline (replaces the current bottom-bar selection list for district choice).
  - Quantity entry: TBD (see open questions).
  - View-only mode: separate keypress bead.

FLASH (see bead 9a5): While a district row is highlighted in the overlay, that district's squares on the board flash at 0.75s period. Flash stops when user confirms or closes overlay.
design:
DEFAULTS
- Cursor position on open: remember last row used (TUI-local state, not engine state); fallback to first row on first open of the session.
- Default qty shown in bottom bar:
    * Buy: min(max_affordable_by_cash, 99)  — 99 is the per-district hard cap.
    * Sell (normal): blank.
    * Sell (forced liquidation): min qty needed to bring ready_cash >= 0, OR total held in that district if that is still insufficient.
- curr->next lines update live, per keystroke, as user edits qty.

ESCAPE LAYERS
- Escape from bottom-bar qty input (after Space-selected a district): unselect district, return to district navigation. Does NOT cancel the whole flow.
- Escape from district navigation (no district selected): cancels — closes overlay; buy = Skip/None; sell from PRE_ROLL = back to PRE_ROLL; view = just close.

DISTRICT ELIGIBILITY
- All districts always rendered in the table.
- In BUY mode, districts not in the server's eligible 'stocks' list are still navigable but refuse on Space (no-op / log).
- In SELL mode, districts with 0 held are still navigable but refuse on Space.
notes: OUT OF SCOPE: LIQUIDATION integration — deferred to bead road_to_riches-ewn. The 'default qty during forced liquidation' behavior documented in design is the target, but wiring it up is a follow-up task.
title: Implement stock market overlay view (interactive)

close_reason: All 3 parts implemented: execute() returns follow-ups, orchestration migrated, log_message() added
description:
## Motivation

Currently, when an event needs to enqueue follow-up events, the only way to do it is through a dispatch handler in game_loop.py's _dispatch() method. This forces ALL orchestration logic into the game loop, even when it has nothing to do with I/O. The result is a massive _dispatch() routing table that mixes three concerns:

1. **I/O** — events that need PlayerInput (Init* events, TurnEvent, WillMoveEvent). These legitimately belong in the game loop.
2. **Orchestration** — events that just enqueue follow-ups (EndTurnEvent, BankruptcyCheckEvent, AdvanceTurnEvent). These have no reason to be in the game loop.
3. **Logging** — leaf mutation events that only log their results (BuyShopEvent, PayRentEvent, etc.). These are boilerplate.

The root cause: execute() takes only (self, state) and returns None, so events cannot declare follow-ups. The game loop is the only code with pipeline access, making it the dumping ground.

This also causes a concrete bug: BankruptcyCheckEvent.execute() calls BankruptcyEvent(...).execute(state) inline because it can't enqueue it. That BankruptcyEvent never appears in pipeline history.

## Solution: execute() returns follow-up events

### Part 1: Pipeline infrastructure

**Step 1: Change GameEvent.execute() signature**
- Base class: `def execute(self, state: GameState) -> list[GameEvent] | None`
- Default implementation returns None
- Events that produce follow-ups return a list

**Step 2: Update EventPipeline.process_next()**
- After calling event.execute(state), collect the return value
- If follow-ups returned, enqueue_front them **in order** (iterate reversed list, calling enqueue_front for each, so they end up in the correct order at the front of the queue)
- Follow-ups always go to the front. Rationale: follow-ups are immediate consequences of the current event and should process before anything already queued. Analysis of every current case confirms front-only is correct — there is no case where follow-ups should go behind existing queue contents.

### Part 2: Migrate orchestration events

Move follow-up enqueueing from dispatch handlers into execute() for these events:

**EndTurnEvent.execute()** → return [BankruptcyCheckEvent, StockFluctuationEvent, TickStatusesEvent, GameOverCheckEvent, AdvanceTurnEvent]. Handler in _dispatch becomes empty and can be removed.

**BankruptcyCheckEvent.execute()** → if check_bankruptcy() is true, return [BankruptcyEvent(player_id)]. Do NOT call BankruptcyEvent.execute() inline anymore. The pipeline will process it naturally. Handler becomes logging-only (log "went bankrupt").

**AdvanceTurnEvent.execute()** → call state.advance_turn(), then return [TurnEvent(player_id=state.current_player.player_id)]. Handler becomes notify-only.

**RollEvent** → currently handler enqueues WillMoveEvent. execute() could return [WillMoveEvent(...)]. But handler also does dice animation logging / I/O, so the handler stays for I/O — just remove the enqueue part.

### Part 3: Migrate logging to log_message()

**Step 3: Add log_message() to GameEvent base class**
- `def log_message(self) -> str | None` — returns None by default
- Called after execute(), so it can read results/fields set during execution
- Events override to return their log string

**Step 4: Update the main loop**
- After _dispatch(event), check event.log_message()
- If non-None, call self.log.log(message) and self.input.notify(self.state, self.log)
- This is a generic mechanism — no dispatch entry needed per event

**Step 5: Migrate leaf events**
Move logging from dispatch isinstance blocks into log_message() overrides on each event class:
- BuyShopEvent, BuyVacantPlotEvent, ForcedBuyoutEvent, InvestInShopEvent
- BuyStockEvent, SellStockEvent, RenovatePropertyEvent, WarpEvent
- SellShopToBankEvent, AuctionSellEvent
- PayRentEvent (conditional + dividends — log_message returns None if rent is 0)
- PayCheckpointTollEvent (conditional — returns None if toll is 0)
- PayTaxEvent (conditional)
- PromotionEvent (reads player state for level — execute() sets this, so log_message() can read it)
- BankruptcyCheckEvent (log if went bankrupt)
- StockFluctuationEvent (log each price change — may return multi-line or joined string)

Special cases that STAY in dispatch:
- VictoryEvent — sets self.game_over and self.winner on the game loop instance. Could use log_message() for the log string but the game_over mutation must stay in dispatch.
- GameOverCheckEvent — same, sets self.game_over and self.winner.

**Step 6: Clean up _dispatch()**
Remove all isinstance blocks that are now handled by follow-ups or log_message(). The remaining dispatch entries should be ONLY:
- I/O events (TurnEvent, WillMoveEvent, Init* events, VentureCardEvent, etc.)
- Game loop control flow (VictoryEvent, GameOverCheckEvent setting game_over)
- TickStatusesEvent pass (or remove entirely since it has no handler)

### Part 4: Update tests
- Update any tests that check execute() return values or mock execute()
- Verify pipeline correctly processes follow-ups by checking event history
- Run full test suite

## Order of operations
Do Part 1 first (infrastructure), verify tests pass. Then Part 2 (orchestration migration), verify. Then Part 3 (logging migration), verify. Each part is independently shippable.
title: Event follow-ups: let execute() return follow-up events

Minimum 140x35 terminal.

description:
When a player is highlighted over a district row in the stock overlay (view/buy/sell modes), the board squares belonging to that district flash their borders at a 0.75s period. Flashing stops as soon as the user confirms the buy/sell or closes the overlay.

SCOPE: TUI only. The original 'camera pan animation' concept from the gameplay spec (§168) is deferred to the future real client (out of TUI scope). Flashing is the TUI equivalent feedback.

DEPENDS ON: road_to_riches-4v8 (overlay view provides the highlight state that drives the flash).
notes:
TRIGGER: cursor hover on a district row in the overlay (all modes: view/buy/sell). No explicit select required to start flashing.
STOP: when cursor moves to another district (flash follows cursor), or overlay closes, or user commits the buy/sell.
STYLE: invert the border colors of all board squares belonging to that district. 0.75s period.
title: Flash district squares while highlighted in stock overlay

description: Rewrite playerstate.py from starter_code. PlayerState with player_id, position, from_square, ready_cash, level, suits dict, owned_properties list, owned_stock dict, statuses list. Part of Core Data Models epic.

description:
Bind key Q (if not taken) to open the stock market overlay in view-only mode. Available at any input-accepting time (not just PRE_ROLL).

MODES (same overlay, different permitted actions):
  - View-only: any input time. No buy/sell permitted.
  - Sell: only when opened from PRE_ROLL's Sell Stock option.
  - Buy: only when server has issued a BUY_STOCK InputRequest.

Closing with Escape returns to the prior input prompt without consuming it.

DEPENDS ON: road_to_riches-4v8 (overlay itself).
notes:
KEYBIND: Q (confirmed not taken in current PRE_ROLL menu or input handlers).
AVAILABILITY: any input-accepting state. Opens overlay in view-only mode (no Space-select allowed).
CLOSE: Escape returns to whatever input prompt was active, without consuming it.
title: Keypress to open stock overlay anytime (view-only)

Missing coverage for: get_liquidation_options (building the options dict), check_victory, VictoryEvent.execute, and the full BankruptcyEvent liquidation flow (releasing properties, selling stock). These are endgame-critical paths.

description:
A standalone AI client process that connects via WebSocket and plays automatically.

## Strategy

### Pathfinding (CHOOSE_PATH)
- BFS optimal tour: compute shortest path visiting all uncollected suit squares then bank for promotion.
- Recompute each turn.
- Ignore change-of-suit squares (only use static suit squares).
- At each intersection, choose the direction that follows the optimal tour.

### Shop buying (BUY_SHOP)
- Always buy empty shops when affordable.

### Stock buying (BUY_STOCK)
- At bank/stockbroker, buy max affordable stock in district with highest total max_capital across ALL players' shops.
- 1.5x bonus multiplier for districts where AI owns shops.

### Investment (INVEST)
- Invest max possible into the owned shop with the lowest current value.
- Prioritize shops in districts where AI owns stock.

### Cannon target (CANNON_TARGET)
- Choose the target player whose position brings the AI closest to its next promotion path target (next uncollected suit or bank).

### Vacant plot type (VACANT_PLOT_TYPE)
- Randomly choose between checkpoint and tax office.

### Auction bids (AUCTION_BID)
- Bid only if AI already owns a shop in that district.
- Bid up to 3x the shop value.

### Liquidation (LIQUIDATION)
- Sell stock first (arbitrary district order), then shops if needed.

### Pre-roll (PRE_ROLL)
- Always roll. No stock selling or trading pre-roll.

### Other defaults
- Confirm stop: always confirm (BFS already picked the right path).
- Forced buyout: always skip.
- Trades/offers: always reject.
- Renovation: skip.
- Configurable response delay (default ~0.5s) so human can follow.

## Implementation
Standalone script in src/road_to_riches/ai/basic/client.py, spawned by the server as a subprocess.
title: Basic AI client (greedy suit collector)

description: Build the P0 TUI using Python Textual library. Includes: command line + scrollable log (bottom 8-10 rows), player input with validation, info command system (info player/square/game), dice display (7x7 or 5x5 with animation). Terminal minimum 140x35. This is the minimum viable client.

Also forced buyouts: after paying rent, option to buy shop for 5x value (owner gets 3x, 2x to bank).

close_reason: Resolved by road_to_riches-dmx: turn lifecycle is now fully event-driven
description: The normal player turn (dice roll, movement, passing squares, landing) appears to be hardcoded in the game loop rather than implemented as GameEvents per the technical spec. The spec shows ROLL, WILL_MOVE, MOVE, PASS_SQUARE_ACTION, STOP_ACTION, END_TURN as events in the queue. Need to audit what's hardcoded vs evented and identify what needs to change.
notes: AUDIT RESULTS: The entire turn lifecycle (TURN, ROLL, WILL_MOVE, MOVE, PASS_ACTION, STOP_ACTION, END_TURN) is hardcoded as procedural control flow in GameLoop._run_turn() and TurnEngine. Only leaf state mutations (BuyShopEvent, PayRentEvent, etc.) are proper GameEvents. The spec requires ALL of these to be events in a queue. This is a significant architectural gap that prevents: (1) scripts from yielding ROLL to trigger normal movement, (2) event history/replay for turns, (3) AI event-queue simulation. Fixing this requires a major refactor of TurnEngine and GameLoop.
title: Audit: game loop hardcodes turn logic instead of using GameEvents

Missing coverage for: add_square_status, add_player_status, tick_player_statuses, tick_board_statuses, get_rent_modifier, is_shop_closed, get_player_commission. These affect rent calculations and shop behavior.

close_reason: Bead 1-3 complete: 7 lifecycle events, dispatch loop, scripts yield GameEvents, TurnEngine deleted, all 175 tests pass
description: MISSION CRITICAL. The entire turn lifecycle is currently hardcoded as procedural control flow in GameLoop._run_turn() and TurnEngine. The spec (design/technical.md) requires ALL turn phases to be GameEvents in a queue: TURN, ROLL, WILL_MOVE, MOVE, PASS_SQUARE_ACTION, STOP_ACTION, END_TURN, STOCK_BUY_SELL_ADJUSTMENT. Only leaf state mutations (BuyShopEvent, PayRentEvent, etc.) are currently proper GameEvents. This requires a major refactor of TurnEngine and GameLoop to be fully event-driven. Once done, ExtraRoll and RollForEvent ScriptCommands can be replaced with real GameEvents (ROLL, ROLL_FOR_EVENT) that scripts yield directly. Read design/technical.md thoroughly before implementing — follow it exactly.
title: Refactor: Event-driven turn lifecycle per technical spec

description: When a player lands on a venture/suit/change-of-suit/suit-yourself square, an 8x8 grid displays. Unclaimed cells show grey '?'. Claimed cells show the claiming player's color and number. The player picks an unclaimed cell (WASD + Space in TUI). After claiming: 1) Check for line bonuses through the newly claimed cell (horizontal, vertical, diagonal). 4-in-a-line=40G, 5=50G, 6=60G, 7=70G, 8=200G. Bonuses stack (extending a 4-line to 5 awards both 40+50=90G). Bonuses are awarded and logged BEFORE the card is drawn. 2) A random card is drawn from the deck (without replacement). Card name+description displayed in log. 3) Grid disappears, then the card script executes. Grid is shared across all players — once claimed, a cell cannot be reclaimed. When all 64 cells are claimed, the entire grid resets. Grid state (claimed cells + remaining deck) must be serialized for save/resume. TUI: show as a smaller widget (not full overlay), pick with WASD, Space to confirm. AI: first pick is random, then try to build lines off existing claims for bonus gold.
title: Implement venture grid (8x8 claim board)

close_reason: Implemented VentureDeck model, card loading from cards/ directory, optional board JSON deck composition, draw with reshuffle, serialization for save/resume, integrated into GameLoop init and _handle_venture_card.
description: Create the venture card deck system. Cards are stored in a cards/ directory. Each card is a subdirectory named by ID (001-100) containing: a) xxx.py — the script (generator function per ScriptEvent pattern), b) a .txt file whose filename is the card name and contents are a short description (7-10 words, addressing player directly with excitement). The deck is defined in the board JSON as an optional field (e.g. venture_deck: [1, 1, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5] — duplicates control frequency). If omitted, the default deck contains all cards found in the cards/ directory. The deck is shuffled at game start. Cards are drawn without replacement. When all cards are exhausted, the entire deck is reshuffled. Deck state (remaining cards) must be part of GameState for save/resume serialization. When a card is drawn, its name and description are shown to ALL players in the log.
title: Implement venture card deck system

description: Create base StatusEffect class with type, modifier/level X, remaining turns. Auto-decrement at end of turn cycle. Remove when expired. Support player statuses and shop statuses. Part of Status Effects epic.

close_reason: 56 tests added covering: GameLog, GameLoop init, TurnEvent, RollEvent, WillMoveEvent, MoveEvent, undo, EndTurnEvent, StopAction, all Init* handlers (buy shop, vacant plot, forced buyout, invest, buy/sell stock, renovate, cannon), stock validation, game over, liquidation, and full turn integration
description:
## Motivation

game_loop.py is the core orchestrator with 779 lines and 0% test coverage. It contains the main event loop, _dispatch routing, all lifecycle event handlers (_handle_turn, _handle_roll, _handle_will_move, _handle_move, _handle_pass_action, _handle_stop_action), Init* event handlers, movement helpers (undo, snapshots), venture card/script runner integration, and liquidation flow. This is the highest-risk untested code in the project.

## Test Framework: unittest.mock.create_autospec

Use `create_autospec(PlayerInput, instance=True)` to mock the PlayerInput interface. This was chosen over two alternatives:

1. **FakePlayerInput base + lambda overrides**: No call tracking (can't assert call counts or args), no sequencing (same lambda for every call), lambdas bypass type checking. Essentially hand-rolling what unittest.mock already provides.

2. **Strict base class + mock.patch.object**: Clean errors on unexpected calls, but verbose per-test setup with nested context managers.

`create_autospec` gives us:
- Zero boilerplate — no FakePlayerInput class to maintain
- `return_value` for simple cases, `side_effect=[val1, val2, ...]` for sequenced responses (e.g. choose_path called 3 times with different results)
- Free call tracking: `assert_called_once_with(...)`, `call_args_list`, `call_count`
- `spec=PlayerInput` prevents mocking methods that don't exist on the interface
- Unconfigured methods return MagicMock (not a clean error), but downstream code fails anyway since MagicMock won't compare equal to expected strings/ints. Minor tradeoff.

## Test categories (highest to lowest value)

1. **Full turn lifecycle**: Enqueue TurnEvent, configure mock to roll/pick paths/confirm stop. Assert pipeline history contains expected event sequence.
2. **Movement + undo**: Move 2 steps then undo. Assert position/cash/from_square restored. Assert retract_log called.
3. **Init* handlers**: For each (buy shop, invest, buy stock, etc.), set up state, configure mock to accept, assert mutation event in pipeline history.
4. **Pass/stop action sequencing**: Assert auto_events from SquareResult enqueued in correct order relative to WillMoveEvent (pass) and EndTurnEvent (stop).
5. **Venture card execution**: VentureCardEvent with a real card script, assert script commands flow correctly.
6. **Liquidation loop**: Player in negative cash, trigger EndTurnEvent, assert choose_liquidation called.
7. **Game over**: Enough bankruptcies, assert game_over flag and winner set.

## File structure

Single file `tests/test_game_loop.py` with test functions grouped by category. Each test creates its own `create_autospec(PlayerInput)` and configures only the methods relevant to that test.
title: Add tests for game_loop.py (0% coverage)

title: Design board editor UI and workflow

description: The spec says events should be sent to all clients when popped from the queue. Currently other players follow along via log messages (notify()), which works for gameplay. Structured event broadcast would enable richer UI animations on other players' screens (dice rolling, movement animations, etc.). Low priority since log messages cover the functional need.
title: Broadcast structured events to non-current player clients

description: When player lands on own shop: may invest in any owned shop up to max capital (MaxCap = LUT * BaseVal - CurrentVal). Investment increases current value by amount invested. Part of Property System epic.
title: Implement shop investment

description: Server currently broadcasts all input requests to all clients and accepts response from any. Need to: (1) assign each WebSocket client a player_id on connect, (2) attach player_id to every input_request message, (3) only accept input_response from the client matching that player_id, (4) all clients still receive all events (state_sync, log, dice) for state tracking. This is a prereq for AI players since AI and human clients must only respond to their own prompts.
title: Per-player input routing in server

description: Implement P1 stock features: Dividends (20% of rent transactions split by stock weight, paid by bank), stock info viewing UI (players x districts table), stock price change camera animation. Players can view stock holdings at any input time during their turn.
title: [Epic] Stock Market P1 Features

close_reason: Bank direction-lock clear now uses ClearDirectionLockEvent instead of raw mutation
description: In _handle_stop_action(), landing on a bank square clears player.from_square = None as a raw state mutation. Should be wrapped in an event for consistency and event history visibility.
title: Bank direction-lock clear bypasses event system

description: engine/property.py computes current_rent, max_capital, and district-level shop counts. 6 statements uncovered. Cover edge cases in rent/capital formulas.
title: Add tests for engine/property.py (84% coverage)

description: Rewrite suit.py and square type enum from starter_code into proper library. Suit enum with SPADE, HEART, DIAMOND, CLUB, WILD and next() method. SquareType enum with all square types. Part of Core Data Models epic.
title: Implement Suit enum and SquareType enum

description: Rewrite gamestate.py from starter_code. GameState with current_player, board_state, stock_state, player_states list, cameo_state list, events. Part of Core Data Models epic.
title: Implement GameState dataclass

description: Create the event queue/pipeline that processes events sequentially, supports chaining (events that trigger other events), and maintains event history for the game log. Part of Event System epic.
title: Implement event execution pipeline

close_reason: EndTurnEvent decomposed into 5 composable sub-events: BankruptcyCheckEvent, StockFluctuationEvent, TickStatusesEvent, GameOverCheckEvent, AdvanceTurnEvent
description: EndTurnEvent.execute() handles bankruptcy, stock fluctuations (STOCK_BUY_SELL_ADJUSTMENT per spec), status ticks, game-over checks, and turn advancement all inline. The spec shows these as separate events in the queue. None of these sub-actions appear in event history, and clients can't animate them individually.
title: Break EndTurnEvent.execute() into composable events

description: Implement P0 stock mechanics: buying stock (at bank/stockbroker/events), selling stock (pre-roll/forced), stock price calculation (value_component + fluctuation_component), price changes on buy/sell >= 10 shares, price updates on property investment. Property component = 4% of total district value.
title: [Epic] Stock Market System (P0)

close_reason: Extracted _log_event helper, called from run(), _execute_event, and _do_extra_roll. PromotionEvent and other leaf events now log correctly during scripts and bonus rolls. Regression test added.
description: log_message() is only called in run() main loop. Events processed via _execute_event (scripts, pre-roll Init handlers) and _do_extra_roll (bonus rolls) skip logging. PromotionEvent triggered during a bonus roll across the bank, or any leaf event from a script, is silently absorbed.
title: Leaf events not logged during _execute_event and _do_extra_roll

description: When the player must choose a square (e.g. teleport destination, investment target), the TUI currently renders a massive list of square IDs as selectable options. This doesn't scale — teleport shows dozens of entries. Instead, prompt the player to type a square ID directly. If invalid, show an error and re-prompt until they enter a valid one. Exception: cannon target selection keeps the current UI (only 3 options, works well). General rule: when there are many valid squares, use text input + validation loop; when there are few fixed options, the current list UI is fine.
title: TUI: Replace long option lists with square ID text input

description: Implement P0 status effect system: Commission X (earn X% of all rent transactions), Closed (shop pays 0 rent), Discount/Price Hike X (shop rent +/- X%). Statuses have duration in turns and auto-expire.
title: [Epic] Status Effects (P0)

description: Implement the event system: GameEvent base class, event registry with @register_event decorator, serialization/deserialization, and event execution pipeline. This is the backbone for all game logic.
title: [Epic] Event System Framework

description: Create a separate board editor application for designing board layout files. Should allow placing squares, setting types, configuring waypoints/connections, assigning districts, and exporting to the game's board file format.
title: [Epic] Board Editor (P1)

description: Implement all P0 square type behaviors: Bank (stock buying, promotion, victory), Shop (buy/rent/invest), Suit (collect suit + venture card), Venture (draw venture card), Take a Break (close shops), Boon (20% commission), Roll On (roll again). Each square has pass and land events.
title: [Epic] P0 Square Types

description: Cover _handle_liquidation_phase (~100 lines) including sell phase, auction phase, and multi-iteration liquidation until cash >= 0.
title: Tests: game_loop liquidation flow (_handle_liquidation_phase)

description: Add a dev/debug command that undoes the previous player's entire turn, restoring the game state to the start of that turn. Requires snapshotting full GameState (board, players, stock, pipeline history) at turn start. The existing undo system only handles movement steps within a turn via MoveSnapshot — this needs a deeper full-state snapshot/restore mechanism.
title: Dev command: undo previous turn

close_reason: Created ClaimVentureCellEvent, grid.claim() now goes through event pipeline
description: In game_loop.py _handle_venture_card(), grid.claim(row, col, player_id) directly mutates the venture grid state instead of going through an event. Should create a ClaimVentureGridCellEvent.
title: Venture grid claim bypasses event system

close_reason: WarpEvent.voluntary=True returns PassActionEvent+StopActionEvent follow-ups (used by card 003). Cannon enqueues CollectSuitEvent for suit destinations only. Doorway/backstreet unchanged. 4 new tests, full suite passes (235).
description: WarpEvent currently does not trigger any pass or land actions at the destination. For voluntary warps (venture card 003 Warp Anywhere), both pass and land must fire. For cannon warp, only suit collection if the destination is a suit square. Involuntary warps (doorway, backstreet) remain unchanged.
title: Fix voluntary warp not triggering pass/land actions

description: Commission X: player earns X% of all rent transactions between any players. Closed: shop pays 0 rent when landed on. Discount/PriceHike X: shop rent temporarily +/- X%. Part of Status Effects epic.
title: Implement Commission, Closed, and Discount/PriceHike statuses

description: Change of Suit: pass = grant current suit, rotate to next. Land = venture card. Suit Yourself: land = grant wild card. Boom: land = 50% commission status. Part of P1 Squares epic.
title: Implement Change of Suit, Suit Yourself, and Boom squares

description: engine/lut.py holds lookup tables (e.g. max_cap_multiplier). Only 2 statements uncovered — small cleanup to reach 100%.
title: Add tests for engine/lut.py (82% coverage)

description: The _liquidation_phase() method in game_loop.py is fully implemented but never called. Players with negative cash after rent/tax should be forced to sell assets before the turn ends. Need to trigger it from the appropriate point (likely after StopActionEvent land effects that cause negative cash, or as part of EndTurnEvent).
title: Integrate liquidation phase into game loop
